methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional community-scale AI literacy infrastructure in Richmond, creating the peer networks and distributed learning capacity that could enable deep adoption beyond elite metro enclaves.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - news.vt.edu
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates grassroots capacity-building in historically underserved regions, potentially enabling distributed deep learning rather than platform dependency or concentration in tech hubs.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Broad business adoption of AI in Hampton Roads suggests shallow platform-layer integration spreading across traditional maritime and defense sectors—distribution without depth, fitting the 'software does it for you' pattern of vendor-driven capability.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI trainer emphasizing worker leadership over technology followership in Roanoke signals pedagogical focus on agency and deep understanding rather than passive tool use, critical for distributed deep adoption in non-metro regions.

signals.title: Northern Virginia Technology Council Urges Removal of Data Center Tax Repeal from State Budget - PR Newswire
signals.signal_reading: NoVA Technology Council's lobbying to protect data center tax incentives reinforces infrastructure concentration in the state's wealthiest corridor, deepening the capital-intensive capability gap between Northern Virginia and rural regions.
